findings.message: Independently flagged by both reviewers. hmac.compare_digest raises TypeError when a str argument contains non-ASCII characters; Werkzeug decodes header values latin-1, so an X-API-Key with a high byte (e.g. 'é') reaches the auth gate and raises, surfacing as a generic 500 instead of the contracted 403. It still fails closed (no bypass, no state change) — robustness, not a hole.
findings.remediation: Compare on bytes to make the check total: hmac.compare_digest(provided.encode('utf-8'), expected.encode('utf-8')); or wrap the compare and treat any exception as an auth failure (403).

findings.message: types-pyyaml is a dev dependency but nothing in src/ or tests/ imports yaml. It is a leftover from the profile seed and adds noise to the intentionally minimal dependency surface.
findings.remediation: Drop types-pyyaml from the [dependency-groups] dev list.

findings.message: Runtime deps use lower-bound floors only (flask>=3.0, waitress>=3.0) with no upper cap and no committed lockfile, so a fresh install is non-reproducible and could pull a breaking/compromised future release. Confirmed by both the verify audit and this review.
findings.remediation: Commit uv.lock (`uv lock`) and consider compatible-release caps (flask>=3.0,<4; waitress>=3.0,<4).

findings.message: No Flask MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH is set, so POST /api/codes buffers the full request body via get_json before validating. API-key gated (authenticated-only resource exhaustion, not a public DoS); waitress's ~1GB default is the only bound. Defense-in-depth.
findings.remediation: Set app.config['MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH'] to a small limit (e.g. 16*1024) in create_app so oversized bodies get 413 before parsing.

findings.message: Referer/User-Agent from the public redirect are stored verbatim (api.py:131-132) and Referer is echoed in the stats JSON (top_referers). No XSS today (application/json, no HTML UI); latent stored-XSS only if a future admin HTML dashboard renders these unescaped.
findings.remediation: Keep serving as JSON; HTML-escape at render time if a UI is ever added; optionally length-cap stored header values.
